data.title: Agri Picks Report April 17, 2025: Geojit Financial Services
data.description:
Geojit Financial Services's report on Daily Agri Picks

Sowing of summer crops in Gujarat progressed to 1.2 million hectares as of Tuesday, up 6.5% from 1.1 million hectares a year ago, according to a release by the state agriculture department. Paddy acreage rose nearly 35% to 128,150 hectares from 95,043 hectares a year ago. The area under bajra fell to 314,597 hectares from 315,713 hectares, the report showed. On the other hand, the acreage of maize was 7,470 hectares, up from 7,085 hectares. Moong acreage in the state so far was 58,894 hectares, up from 45,592 hectares a year ago. Similarly, the area under urad rose to 30,195 hectares from 21,485 hectares a year ago. The acreage of sesame stood at 121,275 hectares, up from 114,986 hectares a year ago, the report showed. The area under groundnut fell to 56,103 hectares from 59,887 hectares a year ago. Sugarcane acreage rose to 12,563 hectares from 10,003 hectares a year ago, according to the report. The area under vegetables in the state stood at 104,925 hectares, up from 102,907 hectares a year ago, while onion agreage was 10,944 hectares, against 10,860 hectares a year ago. In Gujarat, summer crops are sown in February and March, and harvested in May and June. Bajra, paddy, sesamum, and moong are the main crops grown in the state during the summer.

For all commodities report, click here

data.title: Mesoblast Expands Insurance Coverage for Ryoncil to 104 Million Lives in US
data.description:
Mesoblast expanded its coverage for Ryoncil to 104 million US government- and private-insured lives, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Ryoncil is approved by the US Food and Drug Administration for mesenchymal stromal cell therapy.

It is the only approved therapy for steroid-refractory acute graft versus host disease (SR-aGvHD) in children aged two months and older, a separate filing showed.

Mesoblast's shares were up nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Germany's Uniper Secures LNG Supply Deals from Woodside
data.description:
Uniper secured sale and purchase agreements for the supply of liquified nitrogen gas from Woodside, according to a Thursday release.

The German energy group will receive 1 million tonnes per annum of LNG from Woodside's Louisiana LNG unit for up to 13 years on a free-on-board basis, and 1 Mtpa of LNG from the latter's global portfolio by Woodside Energy Trading Singapore until 2039.

The agreements are subject to a final investment decision by Woodside for the Louisiana LNG project.

data.title: China Tightens EV Battery Safety Rules
data.description:
China's updated national standard for electric vehicle battery safety, GB38031-2025, will enforce stricter testing starting from July 2026, Xinhua News reported Tuesday.

Key revisions include refined thermal diffusion tests, a new bottom impact test to assess crash protection, and a fast-charge safety check requiring batteries to withstand 300 rapid cycles without fire or explosion, according to the report.

The rules apply to propulsion power batteries, excluding non-propulsion ones, Xinhua News wrote.

data.title: Yamatane Corp - To Conduct 2-For-1 Share Split For Shareholders As Of May 31
data.description:
Yamatane Corp :

  • YAMATANE CORP - TO CONDUCT 2-FOR-1 SHARE SPLIT FOR SHAREHOLDERS AS OF MAY 31

data.title: Clarity Pharmaceuticals Secures AU$11.1 Million Tax Incentive Refund; Shares Up 12%
data.description:
Clarity Pharmaceuticals received an AU$11.1 million research and development tax incentive refund, inclusive of interest, as part of the Australian Federal Government's R&D Tax Incentive program, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The refund will support the company's further development of its Targeted Copper Theranostic (TCT) platform of products for various cancer indications.

The company has decided to prioritize the development of its two products related to diagnostic and therapeutic applications for prostate and breast cancer.

To focus on the aforementioned products, the company has closed trials for two of its prostate cancer and neuroblastoma-related products.

The company's shares rose 12% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: China to Issue 12.5 Billion Yuan in Treasury Bonds in Hong Kong
data.description:
China's Ministry of Finance on April 23 will issue 12.5 billion yuan in bonds in Hong Kong, the same amount as the previous Feb. 19 issuance, Xinhua News reported Tuesday.

This marked the second batch of renminbi-denominated treasury bonds issued in the administrative region this year.

data.title: India's Sensex Falls 0.4%, Dragged by Tech, Industrial StocksMarket Talk
data.description: Indian shares are lower as concerns about U.S. trade policy persist. The Sensex falls 0.4% to 76719.16. Tech and industrial stocks are leading declines. Wipro is down 5.0% following 4Q results. HCL Technologies declines 2.8% and Tech Mahindra is 1.1% lower. Tata Steel drops 1.9% and Larsen & Toubro loses 1.5%. Among individual movers, Hero MotoCorp declines 2.7% after it suspended production at four plants for three days. Investors are focusing on U.S. tariff news as well as domestic earnings. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Bank of Korea's Policy Seen as Dovish Despite On-Hold DecisionMarket Talk
data.description: The Bank of Korea's policy stance is seen as remaining dovish amid growing growth concerns, despite its latest decision to hold interest rates steady, ING economist Min Joo Kang writes in a note. Heightened trade uncertainty, volatile currency markets, and rising household debt made the BOK reluctant to move in April, Kang says, but a rate cut in May is likely. The ING analyst takes note of one dissenting board member calling for a rate cut at Thursday's policy meeting. All board members are also open to a further cut in the next three months, she adds. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Ace Software Exports Invests INR40 Million in Wholly Owned Subsidiary
data.description:
Ace Software Exports has invested 40 million Indian rupees in its wholly owned subsidiary, AQE Tecthtools, according to a BSE filing on Wednesday.

The investment was made through the subscription to the rights issue of 400,000 equity shares of AQE Tecthtools of face value 10 rupees each, fully paid up.

Shares of the company were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Maxio Technology Hangzhou Swings to Loss in Q1; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Maxio Technology Hangzhou swung to a net loss attributable to shareholders of 24.8 million yuan or 1.45 yuan per share in the first quarter, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

The Chinese semiconductor company's attributable profit a year earlier was 10.2 million yuan or 1.93 yuan per share.

The company's operating income rose 11% to 241.1 million yuan from 216.8 million yuan in the prior year, the filing said.

The company's shares slipped less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Prescient Therapeutics Receives Fast Track FDA Designation for Potential Lymphoma Treatment; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Prescient Therapeutics received a fast-track designation from the US Food and Drug Administration for its PTX-100 compound for the potential treatment of adults with relapsed or refractory mycosis fungoides, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The designation will expedite the review of therapies for serious conditions with high unmet need, enabling earlier delivery to patients, the filing said.

Mycosis fungoides is a subtype of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma, a group of rare blood cancers affecting the skin.

Shares rose nearly 5% in afternoon trade Thursday.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea to Boost Support for Food and Agricultural Exports Amid US Tariffs
data.description:
South Korea's Agriculture Minister, Song Mi-ryung, announced that the government will increase support for food and agricultural exporters in response to growing trade uncertainties, particularly following US reciprocal tariffs.

Song, speaking at a food export promotion fair in Seoul, emphasized the importance of market diversification to adapt to rapidly changing export conditions, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

The fair, which features 123 buyers from 43 countries and 230 local food companies, comes amid concerns triggered by US tariffs on South Korea.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: China's former agriculture minister faces graft charges
data.description:
China's former agriculture minister, Tang Renjian, is facing bribery charges, the nation's top prosecutor said on Thursday, after his high-profile arrest last year in President Xi Jinping's sweeping anti-graft campaign.

Tang, also former secretary of the ruling Communist Party in the ministry, was removed from the website roster of its leadership last May and expelled from the party six months later.

The charges, filed in the northeastern province of Jilin, accuse Tang of using his high-ranking positions to seek benefits for others and illegally accept "especially large sums" of money and property, the Supreme People's Procuratorate said.

It did not specify the exact amount involved.

Reuters could not reach Tang to seek comment.

From 2017 to 2020, Tang was governor of the northwestern province of Gansu before becoming minister, official biographies show.

As minister, Tang led efforts to beef up food security, approving the use of genetically-modified crops and rolling out a food security law.

data.title: Hong Kong stocks rise, led by property and tech shares
data.description:
Hong Kong shares rose on Thursday, led by property, tech and chip stocks, while investors awaited more cues on Sino-U.S. trade tensions. China equities were broadly steady.

** Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng rose 1.6%. China's blue-chip CSI 300 Index was flat by the lunch break, while the Shanghai Composite Index inched up 0.2%.

** Property shares traded onshore and offshore surged nearly 3% each, after China's premier urged unlocking real estate market potential and new home prices stabilised in March.

** Tech majors traded in Hong Kong rose 2.3%, reversing some losses from the previous day after the United States restricted Nvidia chip sales to China.

** China will pay no attention if the U.S. continues to play the "tariff numbers game", China's foreign ministry said on Thursday, after the White House outlined how China faces tariffs of up to 245% due to its retaliatory actions.

** "U.S. and China are stuck in an unprecedented, and expensive, game of chicken, and it seems both sides are unwilling to back down," said Ting Lu, chief China economist at Nomura.

** "We expect tensions between these two mega economies to worsen significantly, especially as China has been making large strides in high-tech sectors, including AI and robotics," Lu said.

** China's semiconductor shares were up 1%, as investors braced for the results from Taiwan's TSMC due later in the day to show further evidence of the wide-ranging uncertainty in the chip industry spurred by U.S. President Donald Trump's trade policies.

** Chip giant SMIC rose 3.4%.

** Investors are also awaiting a press conference scheduled for Monday, where regulators are expected to outline their plan for "expanding opening-up of the service-sector".

data.description:
IDFC First Bank CEO To CNBC-TV18:

data.title: Alara Resources Dispatches 15th Copper-Gold Concentrate Shipment From Oman Mine
data.description:
Alara Resources dispatched the 15th shipment of copper-gold concentrate from its Al Wash-hi-Majaza mine in Oman, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The copper-concentrate plant produced 1,362 wet metric tonnes of copper-gold concentrate, containing 233 metric tonnes of copper and 3.8 kilograms of gold, in the latest shipping cycle, the filing said.

The mine is operated by the company's joint venture Al Hadeetha Resources, where it holds a 51% stake, per the filing.

data.title: CNP Assurances Closes Sale of Life, General Insurance Unit for EUR182 Million
data.description:
CNP Assurances (CNP.PA) finalized the sale of its life and nonlife insurance business CNP Cyprus Insurance Holdings to Hellenic Bank Public Co. for 182 million euros.

The sale is part of the French insurer's efforts to focus on international expansion, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Response Plus Unit Agrees to Upgrade Aviation Emergency Response in Bahamas
data.description:
Response Plus Holding (ADX:RPM) unit Response Plus Medical Services agreed with Bahamas Aviation, Climate and Severe Weather Network to upgrade the aviation emergency response capacity in the Caribbean island nation.

The medical services company will work with the government to develop and roll out emergency medical services infrastructure for aviation-related accidents and train local professionals, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Maxio Technology Hangzhou's 2024 Profit Soars 126%, Operating Income Climbs 14%; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Maxio Technology Hangzhou's net profit attributable to shareholders for 2024 soared 126% to 118 million yuan from 52.2 million yuan a year ago, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Wednesday.

Earnings per share jumped 113% year on year to 0.32 yuan from 0.15 yuan.

The Chinese semiconductor company's operating income rose 14% to 1.17 billion yuan from 1.03 billion yuan in the prior year, the filing said.

The company's shares slipped less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: OpenAI's Stargate AI venture weighs UK investment, FT reports
data.description: Stargate, funded by SoftBank , OpenAI and Oracle , is weighing a future investment in the UK as it looks overseas to build out global infrastructure for powerful AI models, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing sources close to the matter.

data.title: Nominate your fintech brand for the Finance Magnates Awards.
data.description:
The Finance Magnates Awards are now open for fintech nominations. If you run a fintech company, you can put your name out there and show what you’ve been working on. These awards highlight companies doing great work in the financial world, especially in tech.

Why Nominate Your FinTech?

Nominating your company for the Finance Magnates Awards positions you as a leader in the fintech industry and provides significant exposure to new clients, investors, and partners. Both winners and nominees gain recognition as innovators in technology and financial services, enhancing their reputation and credibility.

Categories Tailored for FinTech

The Finance Magnates Awards feature categories that highlight the diverse aspects of fintech, from prime brokerage services to innovative financial solutions. Each category spotlights the unique contributions of fintech brands to the financial industry.

FinTech Group Nominations (B2B)

  • Institutional Trading: For excellence in providing liquidity, prime brokerage services etc.

  • Services for Brokers: For the best innovations and solutions in brokerage services.

  • Tech for Brokers: For the newest tech advancements helping brokers.

This is your opportunity to shine among the best in the industry. Nominate your Fintech brand now.

Benefits of Participating

  • Brand Exposure Campaign: As a nominee, your company will be featured in a targeted marketing campaign, including press releases, social media shout-outs, and newsletter features, ensuring high visibility across the industry.

  • Industry Recognition: Being recognised by a panel of industry experts can significantly enhance your company's profile.

  • Marketing Advantage: Use the award in your marketing materials, website, and social media to highlight your industry excellence.

  • Networking Opportunities: Attend the awards ceremony on November 6th to network with other top brokerage and fintech players.

Instructions for Nominating Your FinTech Brand

1. Visit the Nominations Page: Visit the Finance Magnates Awards website and navigate to the nominations section. You can access it directly through this link: Nominations Page.

2. Select the Right Category: Once on the nominations form, look for the 'B2B Group Nominations (FinTech)' section. This section contains the fintech-specific categories.

3. Choose Your Subcategory: In the B2B Group Nominations, select the categories that apply to your fintech brand.

4. Fill Out the Form: Provide all the required information about your brand in the form.

5. Submit Your Nomination: After filling out the form, review all the information to ensure accuracy, then submit your nomination.

Join the Ranks of Innovators

This is a great time to get your fintech business in front of the right people. The Finance Magnates Awards 2025 allow you to get noticed, connect with others, and show your innovations to the world.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Thursday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Travelers Stock Climbs on Earnings Beat Despite Massive Losses From California Wildfires

Travelers posts a catastrophe loss in the first quarter of $2.27 billion, wider than the $712 million reported a year earlier.

U.S. Bancorp Profit Climbs With Higher Fees, Lower Costs

The parent company of U.S. Bank posted a profit of $1.71 billion, or $1.03 a share, above analysts expectations of 98 cents a share.

Pro Take: Venture Deal Volume Just Dropped, Suggesting Impact of Tariffs

The dealmaking count declined last week, according to research firm CB Insights, signaling a quick reaction to macroeconomic uncertainty.

Powell Warns of 'Challenging Scenario' for Fed as Trade War Rages

The central bank is wary of a situation where prices rise at the same time that growth slumps.

Bank of Canada Holds Rates Steady as It Examines Tariff Fallout

The Bank of Canada held its main interest rate unchanged at 2.75% after seven consecutive cuts.

Foreign Central Banks Are Loading Up on T-Bills, Selling U.S. Bonds

Every month, the Treasury Department publishes delayed data on U.S. assets sold and bought by foreign central banks and investors.

What the Weak Dollar Means for the Global Economy

The currency's dramatic slide will hit overseas exporters and raise pressure on central banks to cut interest rates.

How a Supreme Court Ruling Could Weaken Fed Independence, Shake Markets

The justices are revisiting "Humphrey's Executor," a decision barring presidents from firing certain federal officials for purely political reasons.

Sagard Buys Stake in Secondary Firm BEX Capital

The Canadian investment firm has grown its asset base by nearly 70 times over the past decade, backing firms with niche private-market strategies.

Nordea Backs Guidance After Earnings Beat

The lender posted net profit attributable to shareholders of EUR1.21 billion for the first quarter, down slightly from a year earlier.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Oil Futures Decouple From Equities to Post Gains

Oil futures defied a tech-led slide in equities and posted gains, held up by a report that China is willing to talk trade with the U.S. under certain conditions.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise for Third Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories rose by 515,000 barrels, while gasoline and distillate stockpiles fell as refineries lowered their capacity use.

IEA Cuts Oil Demand Forecast as U.S. Tariffs Weigh on Economic Growth

Oil demand growth is forecast to be weaker than previously projected as escalating global trade tensions sparked by U.S. President Trump's tariffs weigh on the global economy, the International Energy Agency said.

Europe's Appetite for Wind Power Helps Sandbrook Capital Find an Exit

Sandbrook Capital scored its first exit, a roughly $1 billion deal for a Norwegian service provider for offshore-wind-energy project developers, benefitting from the sector's fast expansion in Europe even as it fell into disfavor in the U.S.

OPEC Cuts Oil Demand Outlook Amid U.S. Tariff Hit

The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries cut its forecast for oil demand growth just days after a surprise decision to boost output, citing the impact of U.S. tariffs on the global economy and crude consumption.

BP Discovers Oil at U.S. Far South Prospect in Gulf of Mexico

Initial data shows high-quality reservoirs and a potentially commercial volume of hydrocarbons, the company said.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Parkland, oil prices and more in the latest Market Talks covering the Energy and Utilities sector.

Trump Has a $350 Billion Deal for Europe: Buy Our Energy

The continent has sharply increased its U.S. purchases in recent years, with little appetite to buy more.

Nuclear Power Is Back. And This Time, AI Can Help Manage the Reactors.

Argonne National Lab has an AI-based tool that can help design and operate nuclear reactors-at a time when AI itself is feeding a power frenzy.

BP Expects First-Quarter Upstream Production to Fall

The company anticipates lower upstream production than the previous quarter due to already announced divestments in Egypt and Trinidad.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0252 GMT - Iron ore rises in the early Asian trading session. While short-term market uncertainty and volatility has decreased, the outlook remains weak, with steel demand declining on-month, Nanhua Futures says in a research note. Expectations of economic stimulus from China is weakening, and prices may see a limited upside given the lack of a driving force, they add. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.6% higher at CNY711.0 a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0245 GMT - Palm oil prices rise in Asian morning trading, tracking gains in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade, says David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Higher palm olein on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is also supporting palm oil prices, he says. The strong export performance, based on cargo surveyors' reports, suggests the price gains could continue, he adds. Ng sees support for crude palm oil prices at 4,000 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,250 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia derivatives contract for July delivery is up 25 ringgit at 4,040 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0221 GMT - Copper is lower amid tariff concerns. Despite China's solid 1Q GDP data, 2Q activity will be affected by the uncertainty in U.S. tariff policy, according to ANZ research analysts in a commentary. Investors' expectations for risks and a potential recession are rising again, Nanhua Futures analysts say, adding that the sentiment may continue to intensify in the short term. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.3% lower at $9,177.00 a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0119 GMT - China's palm oil demand may rise amid the U.S.-China trade tensions as the Asian country seeks alternatives for soybean imports from the U.S., says Hong Leong Investment Bank analyst Chye Wen Fei in a note. However, downside risks from weak global demand and falling crude oil prices could offset any gains in crude palm oil, she says. Lower crude oil prices reduce the appeal of palm oil as biofuel. She maintains her 2025-2026 estimates for crude palm oil prices at MYR4,000 a ton and MYR3,800 a ton, respectively. With palm oil supply recovering and no strong catalysts in sight, Chye keeps a neutral rating on the Malaysian plantation sector. Hong Leong names Johor Plantations, Hap Seng Plantations and IOI Corp. as its top sector picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0026 GMT - BHP reported robust 3Q output and, importantly, has been able to find a way to avoid the sharp reduction in Escondida output beyond FY27, says Citi analyst Paul McTaggart. "We don't have details but it includes extending the life of the oldest concentrator beyond FY29," he says in a note. That's "a good outcome for Escondida following the disappointments of the November site visit last year," McTaggart adds. Citi has a buy rating and A$45.00 target on BHP, which is up 0.5% at A$36.24. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2339 GMT - Gold rises in the early Asian session, buoyed by likely safe-haven demand after Fed Chair Powell warned overnight of economic risks. "The outlook for gold remains constructive, supported by a robust mix of macroeconomic and geopolitical factors that reinforce its intrinsic value as a store of value and diversification asset in uncertain times," Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia says in an email. If current conditions continue, gold's medium- to long-term target is projected around $3,734/oz, the research strategist adds. Spot gold is 0.3% higher at $3,350.88/oz after earlier touching a record intraday high of $3,357.92/oz, according to ICE platform. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2117 GMT - Natural gas demand keeps growing in the U.S., Kinder Morgan says after reporting higher revenue in the 4Q. "The landscape for natural gas continues to be more and more favorable," the company says, adding that for the second year in a row, the U.S. set a first quarter record for demand, which grew by 6.8 billion cubic feet per day compared to the same period a year earlier. "Residential/commercial natural demand and liquefied natural gas feedgas demand were up 10% and 15%, respectively, in the latest quarter," the company says. Shares rise 0.4% to $27.05 in post-market trading. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

2045 GMT - Lean hog futures closed trading up 2.9%, to 97.925 cents a pound, with some traders holding short positions being "flushed out" ahead of the holiday-shortened week, says Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting. "The U.S. pork inventory has been dropping and this is keeping the contract supported as well," said Setzer. Live cattle closed up 1.1%, to $2.02075 a pound. Setzer says traders are preparing for tomorrow's USDA Cattle on Feed report, which is expected to show a decline in the total inventory of cattle on U.S. feedlots. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1951 GMT - Treasury Yields fall for the third consecutive day as Powell says tariffs could make it harder to decide on interest rates. "Fed Chair Powell suggested that patience was the best course," as opposed to cutting rates fast, Global X's Scott Helfstein writes. March retail sales were better than expected, while industrial production fell more than forecast. Weekly jobless claims tomorrow are expected to increase slightly, in a WSJ survey. Bond markets are set to close at 2 p.m. ET tomorrow and the whole day on Good Friday. The 10-year yield declines 0.046 percentage point to 4.276% and the two-year falls 0.043 p.p. to 3.784%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1943 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures fall as the market looks beyond tomorrow's EIA storage report, which is expected to show a below-average injection, to larger inventory builds in subsequent weeks. Still, with the decline in futures pricing over the past month, there's less economic incentive to bring new production online and less potential for gas-to-coal fuel switching in the power generation sector, Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Energy Advisors says in a note. "This dynamic exacerbates fundamental market risk in the coming months and could lead to lighter storage growth than initially expected this summer." Nymex natural gas settles down 2.5% at $3.247/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1936 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures fall as the market looks beyond tomorrow's EIA storage report, which is expected to show a below-average injection, to larger inventory builds in subsequent weeks. Still, with the decline in futures pricing over the past month, there's less economic incentive to bring new production online and less potential for gas-to-coal fuel switching in the power generation sector, Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Energy Advisors says in a note. "This dynamic exacerbates fundamental market risk in the coming months and could lead to lighter storage growth than initially expected this summer." Nymex natural gas settles down 2.5% at $3.247/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1901 GMT [Dow Jones]--Oil futures defy a tech-led slide in equities and post gains, held up by a report that China is willing to talk trade with the U.S. under certain conditions, further losses in the U.S. dollar, and a smaller-than-expected build in U.S. crude-oil stockpiles. "A return to some kind of 'normal' trade relationship between the two largest economies in the world would be a positive price development that could postpone some of the more dire forecasts for oil prices," Mizuho's Robert Yawger says in a note. The Treasury Department, meanwhile, stepped up enforcement against Iran with sanctions against a second Chinese independent refinery for buying what it said was more than $1 billion of Iranian oil. WTI settles up 1.9% at $62.47 a barrel and Brent rises 1.8% to $65.85 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: ECB Rate Decision in Focus
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

ECB rate decision; trading updates from Hermès International, Pernod Ricard, L'Oreal, ABB, J Sainsbury, Rentokil Initial, Deliveroo

Opening Call:

European stock futures were lower despite a positive lead from Asia equity markets as investors awaited the outcome of U.S.-Japan trade talks. The dollar and U.S. Treasury yields edged higher. Oil and gold also advanced.

Equities:

European stock futures were tracking lower ahead of a long weekend and expectations for the European Central Bank to cut its benchmark lending rate by a quarter-point on Thursday.

"The ongoing trade tensions as well as a high level of uncertainty could force the ECB to cut interest rates further than it would currently like to admit," said Carsten Brzeski, ING's global head of macro.

"At the same time, the strengthening of the euro exchange rate, not only vis-à-vis the dollar, will insert more disinflationary pressure on the eurozone. Pre-empting some of this pressure could push the ECB to at least verbally open the door to accommodative monetary policy."

A slide in U.S. stocks turned into a full-blown selloff on Wednesday after Fed Chair Jerome Powell warned of a "challenging scenario" where the central bank could have less flexibility to quickly cushion the economy from the fallout of President Trump's trade war.

Markets in the U.S and Europe will be closed tomorrow in observance of Good Friday.

Forex:

The dollar gained slightly early Thursday, but concerns over its status as the de facto global currency are rising.

While the yen, franc, and euro have shot higher over the past few weeks, the ICE U.S. Dollar Index, a popular gauge of the dollar's value against its main currency rivals, sank to its lowest level in three years.

Trump's policy shake-up not only threatens the dollar's status as a safe haven during times of market stress, it also could erode the dollar's status as the de facto global currency, said Thierry Wizman, global FX and rates strategist at Macquarie Group.

Bonds:

U.S. bond markets will close early on Thursday for Maundy Thursday, or Holy Thursday, and remain closed on Friday for the Good Friday holiday.

U.S. and eurozone credit assets are still attractive, given the healthy state of corporate balance sheets, manageable debt levels, and availability of cash chasing attractive yields, Societe Generale said. There is a risk, however, that trade tensions drive economies into recession, which could hurt credit markets, Societe Generale said.

"The risk of a recession in the EU is small (higher and rising in the U.S.), and unless it is a long or deep recession, we believe that spreads will not react as badly as in previous crises," it said.

Energy:

Oil rose amid prospects for U.S.-China trade negotiations. China has indicated that it's open to initiating trade talks, contingent upon key favourable actions from the Trump administration, two members of Sucden Financial's Research team said.

"Oil futures remain closely tied to the fundamental demand landscape," the members said. China's willingness to engage in talks with the U.S. under favourable bilateral terms has spurred gains in WTI and Brent prices, they added.

Metals:

Gold gained early Thursday, buoyed by likely safe-haven demand after Fed Chair Powell warned of economic risks.

"The outlook for gold remains constructive, supported by a robust mix of macroeconomic and geopolitical factors that reinforce its intrinsic value as a store of value and diversification asset in uncertain times," Pepperstone's Quasar Elizundia said. If current conditions continue, gold's medium- to long-term target is projected around $3,734/oz, the research strategist added.

--

Copper was lower amid tariff concerns. China's 2Q activity will be affected by the uncertainty in U.S. tariff policy, ANZ research analysts said. Investors' expectations for risks and a potential recession are rising again, Nanhua Futures analysts said, adding that the sentiment may continue to intensify in the short term.

--

Iron ore futures rose. While short-term market uncertainty and volatility have decreased, the outlook remains weak, with steel demand declining on-month, Nanhua Futures said. Expectations of economic stimulus from China are weakening, and prices may see a limited upside given the lack of a driving force, they added.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

The Post-Covid Era of Ultra-Calm Markets Is Over

The era of ultra-calm markets is now a distant memory.

Stocks steadily marched higher for much of the past two years, clinching dozens of records. Selloffs were brief and infrequent: Last year, the S&P 500 completed a 356-session streak without a daily decline of 2% or more, its best such stretch since one that ended in February 2007, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

U.S. Tariff Policy Poses Major Risk to Japan's Economy, BOJ Member Says

U.S. trade policy is one of the biggest risks to Japan's economic outlook, Bank of Japan board member Junko Nakagawa warned as she flagged continued uncertainty over tariffs.

"Since the extent of the [economic] impact largely depends on the future course of tariff policies, it is necessary to monitor developments with high vigilance," Nakagawa said in a speech to business leaders in Gunma prefecture, located in the center of Japan's main island.

ECB Is Poised for Seventh Rate Cut. Why It's Easing Faster than the Fed.

Europe is being squeezed as trade tension between the U.S. and China worsens, leaving the European Central Bank with little option but to keep lowering interest rates to support the eurozone economy.

The ECB is expected to reduce its benchmark lending rate by a quarter-point on Thursday. Data on interest-rate futures from the London Stock Exchange Group point to 95% odds of a cut, the seventh since it started lowering borrowing costs in June.

Brunello Cucinelli Reports Revenue Growth, Confirms Guidance

Brunello Cucinelli confirmed its guidance for 2025 and 2026, after it posted an increase in first-quarter revenue.

The Italian luxury-fashion firm booked revenue of 341.5 million euros ($385.3 million), up 10.5% at current exchange rates compared with the year-earlier period.

Taiwan Semiconductor Earnings Are Coming. Guidance Is Key.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing is expected to report a jump in profits for the first quarter, but Wall Street will be paying closer attention to any guidance the company provides as the U.S. trade war with China continues.

The Taiwanese chip manufacturer, also called TSMC, is scheduled to report first-quarter financial results before the stock market opens on Thursday. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect the company to post first-quarter net income of 348.7 billion New Taiwan dollars (about $10.8 billion), which would be a 55% increase from the year-ago period.

BHP's Third-Quarter Copper Production Rises, Iron Ore Output Flat

BHP Group reported a 10% rise in third-quarter copper output due to higher volumes from the giant Escondida mine, and flat iron-ore output as recent improvements to its operations helped offset the impact of tropical cyclones.

BHP, the world's No. 1 miner by market value, said it produced 513,200 metric tons of copper in the three months through March, up 10% on the year-prior period. Production at Escondida, the world's largest copper mine, located in the Atacama Desert of northern Chile, jumped by 16% year over year.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Thursday

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

data.title: Market Chatter: Yen Strengthens Against Dollar Amid US-Japan Trade Talks
data.description:
The Japanese yen surged to the 141 level versus the US dollar on Wednesday, reaching its highest point since September, according to a Nikkei Asia report on Thursday.

The rally comes as traders anticipate discussions between US and Japanese officials addressing the yen's prolonged depreciation, said the news portal.

Japan's Economic and Fiscal Policy Minister, Ryosei Akazawa, held talks with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent later in the day.

Bessent has previously described the yen's recent gains as "market-driven," while former President Donald Trump has accused Japan of deliberate currency devaluation. These contrasting views have fueled market expectations of further yen appreciation, the report said.

Investors are closely monitoring developments, as the currency's movement could influence upcoming trade negotiations between the two nations, added the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Wipro shares drop after weak first quarter forecast flags growth worries
data.description:
Shares of Wipro fell as much as 6.3% on Thursday, a day after the company forecast a weak June quarter, adding to concerns over weak demand flagged earlier by rival TCS .

India's fourth biggest IT exporter on Wednesday forecast revenue in the first quarter to fall between 1.5% and 3.5% sequentially, with Chief Executive Srini Pallia saying "uncertainties have dramatically increased" heading into the new fiscal year.

At least 20 analysts have cut their target price on Wipro's stock, according to data compiled by LSEG.

"Wipro's outlook was weaker than what the market anticipated," said BOB Capital Markets said in a note, adding that a weak start for fiscal 2026 was "probably a harbinger for a third successive year of negative revenue growth".

Shares were trading down 5.6% as of 9.41 a.m. IST.

data.title: Edison Lithium Provides Update on Sale of Interest in Argentinian Lithium Properties
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - April 17, 2025) - Edison Lithium Corp. (OTC Pink: EDDYF) (FSE: VV0) ("Edison" or the "Company") is pleased to provide an update regarding the proposed sale of its Argentina subsidiary, Resource Ventures S.A. ("ReVe"), to Mava Gasoil LLC ("Mava").

Further to its prior news releases on this proposed disposition, the Company has been advised that Mava's legal counsel is finalizing the draft definitive agreement, which is expected to be delivered to the Company by the end of this week. The parties intend to proceed with closing the transaction upon finalization of the definitive agreement and satisfaction of closing conditions.

The Company previously received an initial deposit of USD$100,000 from Mava and further payments of USD$3,200,000, USD$100,000 and USD$100,000 are payable by Mava to the Company and the two other shareholders of ReVe, respectively, upon closing. As previously disclosed, the proposed transaction contemplates Mava acquiring all of the issued and outstanding shares of ReVe for total consideration of USD$3,500,000.

The transaction remains subject to, among other conditions, the negotiation and execution of a definitive agreement, TSX Venture Exchange final acceptance, and receipt of the balance of the purchase price.

About Edison Lithium Corp.

Edison Lithium Corp. is a Canadian-based junior mining exploration company focused on the procurement, exploration and development of cobalt, lithium, alkali and other energy metal properties. The Company's acquisition strategy is based on acquiring affordable, cost-effective, and highly regarded mineral properties in areas with proven geological potential. Edison is building a portfolio of quality assets capable of supplying critical materials to the battery industry and intends to capitalize on and have its shareholders benefit from the renewed interest in the battery metals space.

On behalf of the Board of Directors:

"Nathan Rotstein"

Nathan Rotstein

Chief Executive Officer and Director

For more information please contact:

Tel: 416-526-3217

Email: info@edisonlithium.com

Website: www.edisonlithium.com

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Forward-Looking Disclaimer: This news release contains certain forward-looking statements. All information provided in this news release is as of the date of this news, and the Company undertakes no duty to update such information, except as required under applicable law.

Forward-looking statements in this press release relate to, among other things: the delivery of a draft definitive agreement and timing thereof, the negotiation and execution of the definitive agreement, the receipt of final TSX Venture Exchange approval for the disposition, the closing of the transaction, and the receipt of the purchase price. Actual future results may differ materially. There can be no assurance that such statements will prove to be accurate, and actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements. data.title: Investors in Fluence Energy, Inc. Should Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before May 12, 2025 to Discuss Your Rights - FLNC
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 17, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Fluence Energy, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/fluence-energy-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143525&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1016154&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2feLTOf_rUj6k" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Fluence Energy, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between October 28, 2021 and February 10, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (1) Fluence's relationship with its founders and largest sources of revenue, Siemens AG and The AES Corporation, was poised to decline; (2) Siemens Energy, Siemens AG's U.S. affiliate, had accused the Company of engineering failures and fraud; (3) Fluence's margins and revenue growth were inflated as Siemens and AES were moving to divest; and (4) based on the foregoing, defendants lacked a reasonable basis for their positive statements related to Fluence's battery energy storage business, as well as related financial results, growth, and prospects.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in Fluence Energy stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/9iva" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/fluence-energy-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=143525&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. Levi, Esq.<br/>Ed Korsinsky, Esq.<br/>33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor<br/>New York, NY 10004<br/><a href="mailto:jlevi@levikorsinsky.com" rel="nofollow">jlevi@levikorsinsky.com</a><br/>Tel: (212) 363-7500<br/>Fax: (212) 363-7171<br/><a href="https://pr.report/9ivb" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Levi & Korsinsky, LLP</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/investors-in-fluence-energy-inc-should-contact-levi-and-korsinsk-1016154">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1016154" width="0"/></body></html>
